task_completion.description: Model an elevator car and classify it as an IfcTransportElement.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender/Bonsai Outliner or 3D viewport, a new object representing the elevator car is visible. In the Object Properties panel, the IFC Class is set to 'IfcTransportElement'.

task_completion.description: Set the PredefinedType of the elevator to 'ELEVATOR'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Object Properties -> IFC Attributes panel for the elevator object, the 'PredefinedType' field is explicitly set to 'ELEVATOR'.

task_completion.description: Include a property set with a property name containing 'Capacity' for the elevator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Object Properties -> IFC Property Sets panel for the elevator, there is a custom property set containing a property whose name includes 'Capacity' (e.g., 'LoadCapacity', 'Capacity').

task_completion.description: Model at least two landing doors classified as IfcDoor.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 3D viewport or Outliner, at least two new door objects are visible near the elevator shaft. Their Object Properties must show the IFC Class as 'IfcDoor'. The total number of doors in the Outliner should be at least 7.

task_completion.description: Ensure all new elements are assigned to an IfcBuildingStorey.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Blender/Bonsai Outliner, the newly created elevator (IfcTransportElement) and landing doors (IfcDoor) are nested hierarchically under an 'IfcBuildingStorey' node (e.g., Level 1, Level 2, or similar storey name).

A 'Save As' or 'Export IFC' dialog box in Blender showing the file path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_elevator_retrofit.ifc', or a terminal/file explorer window confirming the file was created at this exact path.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the Blender/Bonsai GUI to perform the modeling and classification tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent actively using Blender's 3D viewport, Outliner, and IFC properties panels, rather than just writing a Python script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely created new elements rather than just renaming existing ones.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original building elements remain intact, and new geometric objects were added for the elevator and doors, rather than simply renaming existing walls or windows to 'Elevator'.

integrity.description: Agent saved a valid IFC file using the software's export/save functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save action was performed through BlenderBIM's 'Save Project' or 'Export IFC' buttons, not by creating a dummy text file with the .ifc extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The initial model (AC20-FZK-Haus.ifc) contains exactly 5 IfcDoor instances. To satisfy the requirement of modeling at least two landing doors, the final model must have at least 7 IfcDoor instances. The elevator must be classified as an IfcTransportElement with the PredefinedType explicitly set to 'ELEVATOR'. A property containing the word 'capacity' (case-insensitive) must be added to the elevator. The final file must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_elevator_retrofit.ifc'.
